title: Vijay clears floor test with 144 MLAs backing TVK: How the high-stakes assembly showdown unfolded
content: Tamil Nadu CM Joseph Vijay's Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam (TVK) successfully passed a crucial floor test, securing the support of several parties including a rebel AIADMK faction. This marks the first coalition government led by a new entrant, breaking the decades-long Dravidian political dominance. Vijay's victory solidifies his position as the state's first non-DMK, non-AIADMK chief minister since 1967.

title: Even Mahabharat would pale: Supreme Court on Kapur family property dispute
content: A protracted property dispute between Sunjay Kapur's mother and wife has prompted the Supreme Court to appoint former CJI D Y Chandrachud as mediator. The apex court noted the legal battle's intensity, comparing it to the Mahabharat. A fresh plea alleges circumvention of a previous order, with the court set to hear the matter further.

title: IPL Playoff Scenarios: GT surge to 99.6%, where do other teams stand?
content: With 14 games left, Gujarat Titans are almost guaranteed a top-four finish with a 99.6% chance, while RCB also has a strong 88.1% probability. Sunrisers Hyderabad and Punjab Kings' playoff hopes are still alive but diminished. Chennai Super Kings and Rajasthan Royals hold a slight edge for the top four, with Kolkata Knight Riders and Delhi Capitals facing slim chances.

title: ‘Removed uterus left in box’: 4 women die after C-section surgery in Kota govt hosp; 6 critical
content: Another woman has died from post-cesarean section complications at Kota's New Medical College Hospital, marking the fourth fatality in a week. Pinki Mahawar, 30, succumbed after being transferred from JK Lon Hospital, with her husband alleging a delayed referral. The incident has led to protests and calls for accountability regarding safety protocols in government hospitals.

title: Microsoft Israel has been placed under the management of Microsoft France, here's why
content: Microsoft Israel's chief has resigned following an internal probe into the misuse of Azure cloud services by the Israeli Ministry of Defense.  The investigation revealed ethical breaches and a lack of transparency, impacting Microsoft's global standing.  This comes after earlier revelations about Unit 8200's surveillance activities, complicating future contract renewals.

title: Gold gets costlier: Why govt raised import duty and what changes for buyers
content: India has significantly increased import duties on gold and silver to 15% from 6%, aiming to curb rising imports and conserve foreign exchange amid the West Asia crisis. This move follows Prime Minister Modi's appeal for austerity and aims to ease pressure on the rupee and the country's balance of payments.

title: Prateek Yadav death case: UP police at BJP leader Aparna Yadav's home, seal husband's room
content: Police are investigating the death of Prateek Yadav, brother of Samajwadi Party chief Akhilesh Yadav, after he was found unconscious at a Lucknow residence. Investigators, including DCP Central Zone Vikrant Vir, visited BJP leader Aparna Yadav's home and seized Prateek's mobile phone. The exact cause of death remains undetermined pending post-mortem and forensic reports.

title: A US family sold their company for $1.7 bn and gave 540 workers $240 mn of it
content: A US-based businessman, Graham Walker, gifted his 540 employees a staggering $240 million from the $1.7 billion sale of his family's company, Fibrebond Corp. This generous act, where workers received an average of $443,000, stemmed from a condition Walker imposed before the sale to power-management giant Eaton.

title: Bengal chief secretary orders crackdown on illegal toll booths in Bengal
content: West Bengal's new BJP government has launched a swift crackdown on illegal toll and drop gates across the state. Chief Secretary Manoj Kumar Agarwal issued the directive, ordering immediate closure of all unauthorized collection points. District administrations are tasked with identifying and removing these illegal operations, with a report due by May 15.

title: Wanted drug lord Prabhdeep Singh extradited to India from Azerbaijan
content: India has successfully extradited fugitive Prabhdeep Singh from Azerbaijan, a key figure in a major international drug investigation. Singh, wanted under an Interpol Red Notice, was brought back by a Delhi Police team. This repatriation, a result of multi-agency efforts, marks a significant win against organized crime and adds to India's growing success in bringing wanted criminals back home.

title: Meta to cut 8,000 jobs and CEO Mark Zuckerberg is making priorities clear for all employees
content: Meta is cutting 8,000 jobs on May 20 as CEO Mark Zuckerberg rebuilds the company around small, AI-powered teams. With capex hitting a record $125–145 billion this year, CFO Susan Li admitted there's no clean answer for what Meta's ideal headcount even looks like anymore. The company wants builders who can ship alone what teams once built together—and is letting everyone else go.

title: Congress leader Sonia Gandhi discharged from Gurgaon's Medanta Hospital
content: Senior Congress leader Sonia Gandhi has been admitted to Medanta Hospital in Gurgaon for a minor procedure. Gandhi, a prominent figure in Indian politics and the widow of former Prime Minister Rajiv Gandhi, has a long and influential history within the Congress party.

title: N Rangasamy takes oath as Puducherry CM for 5th time
content: N Rangasamy has been sworn in as Puducherry's Chief Minister for a fifth term, leading an AINRC-BJP NDA government. He secured victory in two constituencies during the recent assembly elections. The NDA coalition holds a comfortable majority in the 30-member assembly, ensuring a stable government for the Union Territory.

title: Gold price prediction after duty hike: Will price rally of today sustain in coming days?
content: Gold price prediction today: Gold and silver prices are rallying in the domestic market due to the sudden hike in import duties. In the coming days they are expected to align closer to the international prices, says Vedika Narvekar, Research Analyst - Commodities & Currencies, Anand Rathi Shares and Stock Brokers.

title: Report: West Asia war not hurting India inflation yet; flags fuel and crop risks ahead
content: India's retail inflation remains largely unaffected by West Asian conflict, with SBI retaining its FY27 forecast at 4.5%. While imported inflation eased slightly, rising food prices, particularly tomatoes, are a key driver. Future trends hinge on energy price pass-through and summer crop progress, with the RBI flagging upside risks.

title: 'Losses in business can break person': What Akhilesh Yadav said on stepbrother's death
content: Samajwadi Party chief Akhilesh Yadav expressed deep grief over the death of his younger brother, Prateek Yadav, describing him as a hardworking and health-conscious individual. Akhilesh highlighted Prateek's determination to progress through his own efforts and mentioned that financial setbacks can deeply affect individuals. The family will proceed with legal formalities and future decisions collectively.

title: Rs 12 lakh! Cummins fined after GT loss, joins Iyer, Pandya in unwanted list
content: Sunrisers Hyderabad captain Pat Cummins faces a Rs 12 lakh fine. This penalty is for a slow over rate during their IPL 2026 match against Gujarat Titans. The team suffered a significant defeat. This marks the team's first such offense this season. Several other captains have also been fined for similar violations.

title: Air India cuts flights as Iran war drives up costs and hits demand
content: Air India has significantly reduced its flight schedule, cancelling around 200 weekly services from June to August due to soaring jet fuel prices and a depreciating rupee. These cuts follow previous reductions and aim to combat substantial losses. The airline is also exploring cost-saving measures like making meals optional on certain flights and unbundling lounge access for business class passengers.

title: Preity Zinta hits out at 'fake narratives' after fourth straight loss
content: Punjab Kings' IPL 2026 playoff hopes are in jeopardy after four straight losses, a stark contrast to their earlier unbeaten run.  Amidst speculation about team environment issues, co-owner Preity Zinta has strongly refuted 'calculated misinformation' and urged responsible reporting.  The team now faces a critical match against Mumbai Indians to halt their slide.

title: 'I don’t think about Americans’ financial situation': Trump says on Iran war
content: President Trump stated that American financial struggles will not influence his Iran negotiations, emphasizing that preventing Iran from obtaining a nuclear weapon is his sole priority. White House Communications Director Steven Cheung affirmed that Trump's ultimate responsibility is American safety and security, as Iran acquiring a nuclear weapon would threaten all Americans.

title: 'Heavy roster': Another Calcutta HC bench, the third, recuses from hearing RG Kar cases
content: Multiple Calcutta High Court benches have released cases concerning the RG Kar rape and murder due to a heavy roster. The victim's parents seek a further probe, while the convicted accused challenges his punishment, and the CBI pushes for a death sentence. A judicial commission is anticipated for a detailed hearing.

title: Rahul 'strongly dissents' on next CBI director's selection; talks concluded in just 5 minutes
content: Rahul Gandhi registered a strong dissent against the CBI director selection process, calling it a "biased exercise" and a "mere formality." He alleged the government denied him crucial information, including candidate appraisal reports, preventing a meaningful role. Gandhi stated he would not be a "rubber stamp" and boycotted the meeting.

title: 'Pulse completely dropped': What we know about Prateek Yadav’s death so far
content: Prateek Yadav, step-brother of Akhilesh Yadav and husband of BJP leader Aparna Yadav, died at 38 in Lucknow under mysterious circumstances. Brought to Civil Hospital by Aparna's brother, doctors declared him dead on arrival. The exact cause of death is under investigation pending a postmortem examination.

title: Dileep Raj, known for 'Milana,' passes away at 47 after heart attack
content: Kannada film industry mourns the loss of actor and producer Dileep Raj, who passed away at 47 following a heart attack. Raj gained widespread acclaim for his role in the 2007 hit 'Milana' and remained active on social media. His recent performance in 'Nimma Vasthugalige Neeve Javaabdaararu' also garnered critical praise. He was also a dedicated theatre artist.

title: Will MS Dhoni likely travel with CSK squad for must-win game vs LSG?
content: MS Dhoni is reportedly traveling with the Chennai Super Kings squad to Lucknow for their IPL 2026 match against the Super Giants on May 15, despite being sidelined with injury. CSK, currently fifth in the standings, are on a strong comeback trail and aiming for a playoff berth, with Dhoni's potential return fueling anticipation.

title: Akhilesh Yadav's stepbrother, husband of BJP leader Aparna Yadav, Prateek dies at 38
content: Prateek Yadav, 38, stepbrother of Akhilesh Yadav and husband of BJP leader Aparna Yadav, was found dead at Lucknow's Civil Hospital early Wednesday. Doctors declared him brought dead. The younger son of the late Mulayam Singh Yadav, Prateek was not involved in politics. A post-mortem is underway to determine the cause of his demise.

title: Gill’s viral ‘dil abhi bhi sache’ post leaves fans in splits after win over SRH
content: Shubman Gill went viral after Gujarat Titans beat Sunrisers Hyderabad by 82 runs in IPL 2026. Gill posted “Dil abhi bhi sache, aur ghar bhi kache” on Instagram, referencing an old joke about SRH’s poor record against GT. On the field, half-centuries from Sai Sudharsan and Washington Sundar helped Gujarat dominate Hyderabad in Ahmedabad.

title: Distressed over mother’s death, Bengaluru PWD engineer jumps to death from hilltop
content: A 33-year-old Public Works Department engineer ended his life by jumping from Devarayanadurga hilltop in Tumakuru district. The engineer, Manju Prasad, was reportedly upset after his mother's passing last year. He left home on Monday, telling his wife he was going to his office. He visited a relative before proceeding to the hilltop.

title: Congress delays, netizens decide: Kerala CM race sparks online carnival
content: Kerala's social media is buzzing with memes and online polls as the Congress leadership deliberates on the next Chief Minister.  V.D. Satheesan, who led the UDF campaign, is widely favored online, with many users urging a swift decision.  Ramesh Chennithala and K.C. Venugopal are also in contention, but public sentiment, expressed creatively online, leans heavily towards Satheesan.

title: Why Madras high court barred TVK MLA who won by 1 vote from Vijay’s floor test
content: Madras High Court has barred TVK MLA Srinivasa Sethupathi, who won by a single vote in Tirupattur, from participating in the upcoming floor test. The court cited the potential impact of his disputed vote on the state's constitutional governance. The MLA has appealed the order to the Supreme Court.

title: Saudi Arabia launched secret retaliatory airstrikes on Iran during regional war: Report
content: Saudi Arabia conducted unpublicized airstrikes on Iran in late March, marking the first direct military action on Iranian soil in retaliation for attacks within the kingdom. This shift in approach followed Iranian strikes on Gulf Cooperation Council states, leading to an informal de-escalation understanding between Riyadh and Tehran.

title: US imposes visa ban on Indians linked to alleged fentanyl trafficking network
content: The US has imposed visa restrictions on 13 individuals, including the owner of an Indian online pharmacy, KS International Traders, accused of selling illicit fentanyl-laced drugs to Americans. This action follows previous sanctions against Indian nationals and firms involved in trafficking fentanyl and its precursor chemicals, highlighting a joint effort between the US and India to combat drug networks.

title: Asian stocks today: Markets mixed as oil prices, inflation worries weigh on sentiment
content: Asian markets showed mixed trading as AI stock momentum waned, oil prices stayed high, and inflation worries persisted. Japan's Nikkei saw a slight gain, while South Korea's Kospi recovered some losses. Concerns over stalled US-Iran talks and hotter US inflation data contributed to investor caution across the region.

title: Hantavirus outbreak: French woman critically ill, placed on artificial lung support
content: A French woman is in critical condition with hantavirus from a cruise ship outbreak. She is being treated with an artificial lung in Paris. The outbreak has caused multiple infections and deaths. Investigations are underway to find the source of the virus. The cruise ship is returning to the Netherlands for cleaning.

title: Hardik Pandya's social media post goes viral amid availability doubts - Watch
content: Hardik Pandya hinted at his recovery through an Instagram story showing him training at Mumbai’s Reliance facility after missing MI’s last game with back spasms. However, the Mumbai Indians skipper did not travel with the initial squad to Dharamsala for the Punjab Kings clash. MI coach Mahela Jayawardene confirmed Pandya remains under medical observation.

title: Gurgaon shocker: Cousin strangles woman to death, hides body in bed box
content: A 23-year-old woman was allegedly strangled to death by her maternal cousin in IMT Manesar, Gurgaon. The accused, who was seeking work, reportedly hid her body in a bed box. The murder was discovered by a colleague when the victim failed to report for work.

title: Tamil Nadu Government Floor Test LIVE Updates: Vijay-led TVK Government Wins Trust Motion With 144 Votes, 22 Opposed
content: Tamil Nadu Government Floor Test LIVE Updates: A rebel faction of the AIADMK, comprising 30 MLAs, said they will support Vijay in Wednesday's floor test.

title: Opinion: Opinion | The Beijing Visit: Why Does Trump Suddenly Need Xi Jinping?
content: China will factor in the real possibility that in the mid-term elections in November, Trump may lose the House of Representatives and may become a lame-duck president. How far should it invest in an agreement with Trump is the question.

title: Last-Minute Call Brings Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang Into Trump's China Trip
content: US President Donald Trump asked Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang at the last minute to join the trip, said a source familiar with the matter who spoke on condition of anonymity.

title: Trump China Visit Live Updates: Trump Heads To China For High-Stakes Talks With Xi Amid Iran War Shadow
content: Donald Trump's 2-Day China Visit Live Update: During the trip, US arms sales to Taiwan, China's controls on rare-earth exports, and the countries' raucous trade relationships are among the main issues expected to be taken up by Trump and Xi.

title: $29 Billion In 60 Days: Iran War Cost 16% More Than US Estimate
content: The figure, revealed during a budget hearing on Capitol Hill, is about $4 billion higher than the department's previous estimate given two weeks ago.

title: Saudi Arabia Launched Covert Attacks On Iran Amid Middle East War: Report
content: The Saudi attacks, not previously reported, mark the first time that the kingdom is known to have directly carried out military action on Iranian soil and show it is becoming much bolder in defending itself against its main regional rival.

title: Congress' Sonia Gandhi Admitted To Medanta Hospital In Gurugram
content: According to the sources, Sonia Gandhi was admitted to Medanta - The Medicity in Gurugram this morning for a minor surgical procedure.

title: Cut Convoy By 50%, Add More EVs: PM Modi's Austerity Push
content: Other ministers, including Delhi Chief Minister Rekha Gupta and Madhya Pradesh Chief Minister Mohan Yadav, have decided to reduce the number of vehicles in their official convoys.

title: Trainer Aircraft Crash Lands In Baramati Months After Ajit Pawar Plane Accident
content: No serious injuries were reported in the incident, and the trainee pilot, who was the sole occupant of the aircraft, escaped safely following the incident near Gojubavi village adjacent to the Baramati airstrip area, officials said.

title: Kash Patel Denies Allegations of Drinking During Senate Budget Hearing
content: Patel has filed a $250 million lawsuit over the story. The Atlantic has said it stands by its reporting and would vigorously defend against the "meritless lawsuit."

title: Trump Downplays Differences With Xi Over Iran Ahead Of Beijing Summit
content: Trump has been unsuccessfully pressing Xi to use China's considerable leverage to prod Iran to agree to US terms to end the more than 2-month-old war - or, at the very least, reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

title: US Appeals Court Pauses Ruling Against Trump's 10% Global Tariffs
content: Trump imposed the temporary 10-percent duty in February, shortly after the Supreme Court struck down many of his global tariffs.

title: Russia Tests Nuclear-Capable 'Satan II' Missile With 35,000 km Range
content: Putin said Sarmat is capable of suborbital flight, giving it a range of more than 35,000 kilometers (21,700 miles) and an extended capability to penetrate any prospective missile defenses.

title: Prateek Yadav's Complicated Relationship With Father Mulayam, Brother Akhilesh
content: On January 19 this year, Prateek openly accused his wife, Aparna Bisht Yadav, of ruining his family ties and said he would seek a divorce from her at the earliest.

title: Explained: Why India Raised Gold Import Duty, And What It Means For You
content: India's forex reserves have slipped amid the Iran war. By raising gold import duty, India is taking pre-emptive steps to save its forex reserves.

title: GenZ Most At Risk Of Job Scams, 54% Aspirants Ignore Warning Signs: LinkedIn
content: LinkedIn finds that 90 per cent of reported scam attempts try to move candidates away from the platform to private messaging apps.

title: Tamil Nadu Assembly session LIVE: CM Vijay's TVK wins trust vote with 144 ‘ayes’
content: Udhayanidhi leads DMK walkout during vote; commotion in House as AIADMK rebel MLAs extend support to TVK

title: What you need to know about Karnataka’s Rohith Vemula Bill
content: The revised Bill aims to prevent exclusion and injustice while safeguarding the right to education and the dignity of students.

title: PM austerity appeal: State leaders take steps to limit their fuel use
content: Top Ministers from Uttar Pradesh, Delhi, Gujarat, Madhya Pradesh, Rajasthan and Bihar, in addition to numerous BJP leaders, were among those who announced measures to reduce convoy sizes and fuel consumption

title: U.S. to impose visa curbs on 13 people linked to Indian firm over fentanyl
content: KS International Traders generated revenue through trafficking in fentanyl, which President Donald Trump ​has designated as a “weapon ‌of mass destruction”, the State Department said

title: Government doubles import duty on gold & silver, effective rate now at 18.4%
content: Industry players and experts warn that this decision will not only encourage a shift to smuggling but will also have other negative effects on employment. The government has not yet released an official statement on the duty hikes

title: Koppal Mango Fair opens, offers wide variety of the fruit and related products
content: Officials said Koppal district has emerged as an important mango-growing region, with mango cultivation spread across more than 6,500 hectares. Kesar mango, in particular, has gained popularity among growers and consumers

title: Prateek Yadav's death: Condolences pour in from across party lines for Mulayam's son
content: SP leader and Prateek's uncle Shivpal Singh Yadav described the death as "extremely sad" and prayed for peace to the departed soul

title: 'Illegal' structures razed at properties of AIMIM leader accused of sheltering TCS case accused
content: Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar Municipal Corporation issued a notice to Mr. Patel over alleged illegal constructions in the city's Naregaon area, seeking a clarification from him within 72 hours. If no reply is received from Mr. Patel, the corporation has the authority to decide the property's fate, says notice

title: Entrust Devaraja Market and Lansdowne Building restoration to heritage experts: Pramoda Devi Wadiyar
content: Her offer, made some time ago, to restore a portion of the Devaraja Market to prove that the structure could indeed be restored using traditional materials and methods was not accepted

title: Plea in Supreme Court to replace or restructure NTA after NEET-UG paper leak
content: “Direct the reconduct of NEET-UG 2026 under the strict scrutiny of a judicially appointed high-powered committee until an interim oversight committee constituted by the apex court has verified and certified the security of the revised examination process,” the petition sought.

title: Police detain volunteers protesting tree felling near Hyderabad’s KBR National Park
content: Residents and environmental activists have risen up against the tree felling — around the National Park — taken up to make way for seven flyovers and seven underpasses as part of the Hyderabad City Innovative

title: OPT fraud: 10,000 foreign students, including Indians, under U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement scanner
content: “Our nation will not tolerate security threats originating from the foreign student programme,” says ICE’s acting director Todd Lyons

title: INCOIS expands coastal flood monitoring to tackle ‘Kallakkadal’ surges
content: The sudden and powerful swell surges pose a significant threat to fishing communities and coastal infrastructure

title: Haryana municipal corporation poll results: BJP’s mayor candidates ahead
content: In Dharuhera, BJP’s Satyanarayan, alias Ajay Jangra, won against Babu Lal, an Independent candidate, by 6,236 votes, while Congress ended up in third place, as per the result declared on Wednesday.

title: 24x7 water supply to begin soon in Hubballi-Dharwad: Minister
content: At present, even during summers, drinking water is being supplied once every four to five days, as compared to 8, 10, or even 15 days earlier, according to the Minister

title: West Bengal BJP president Samik Bhattacharya says new government will adopt new land policy to bring industries
content: He makes promises to not let any political party ask for money from people who want to open factories

title: Rangasamy takes oath as Puducherry CM for 5th time
content: Two other cabinet members — Malladi Krishna Rao from the regional party and A. Namassivayam of the BJP, also took oath as Ministers in the AINRC-led NDA government

title: PM Modi reduces convoy size: Sources
content: According to sources, the convoy size was reduced in Gujarat and Assam immediately after his speech in Hyderabad

title: Seventy police personnel felicitated for successful anti-terror operation in Jammu & Kashmir's Kathua
content: Kathua SSP Mohita Sharma presided over the function and appreciated the courage, professionalism and dedication displayed by the personnel

title: Sonia Gandhi admitted to Gurugram hospital for minor medical procedure
content: The hospital authorities have not released any details about the leader’s health status or the treatment/ procedure she is to undergo.

content: Referring to the National Education Policy 2020, the Bench noted that the policy recognises that children learn better in their mother tongue and accordingly accords primacy to home, local, and regional languages in early education

title: Jammu and Kashmir leaders ask Omar Abdullah government to follow steps of Tamil Nadu CM Vijay on liquor ban
content: To make a point on how it was not an easy move to ban liquor in J&K, Mr. Abdullah referred to former Prime Minister Morarji Desai’s visit to J&K and his advocacy to ban liquor in J&K in the 1970s

title: Hanuman Jayanti: Muslim residents offer buttermilk to Hindu devotees in Kadapa days after clashes
content: The Collector and SP held negotiations between the two communities and increased police deployment for the procession

title: Extremely saddening: Akhilesh on step-brother Prateek's death
content: He was a very good young man who wanted to achieve something through his own hard work. Sadly, he is no longer among us, says Samajwadi Party president Akhilesh Yadav

title: U.P. BJP announces State-wide protest over Samajwadi Party MP’s comment against PM Modi
content: U.P. CM Yogi Adityanath described the comment as an insult to the trust of 145 crore countrymen and the democratic dignity of India

title: Industries Minister Bharath holds door-to-door campaign in Kurnool; warns Secretariat staff over lapses
content: The Industries Minister conducted a door-to-door campaign in Ward No. 8 as part of the ‘Our City, Our Responsibility’ initiative

title: FIR against Tumakuru madrasa management after 24 runaway children allege abuse and forced labour
content: During an interaction with officials, the children alleged that they had fled the madrasa due to physical assault, harassment, and ill-treatment by staff members

content: “The aircraft was flying at a comparatively low altitude when it developed a technical malfunction, and one one side of the aircraft reportedly hit a light pole before it struck the ground,” Superintendent of Police, Pune, said

content: With Mr. Sinha hardening his stand, the J&K police on Tuesday (May 12, 2026) stepped up actions against the accused in Kashmir

content: Bangla Pokkho is a pro-Bengali advocacy organisation, and Mr. Chatterjee is often found campaigning for prioritising the Bengali language and Bengalis over other languages and communities in West Bengal

content: The philatelic museum, which was set up in January 1997, exhibits commemorative stamps and first-day postal covers issued by India and a host of countries, including those in the African and Caribbean regions on Gandhiji. content: Women have been serving as Ministers in the Tamil Nadu Cabinet for a long time. Even before India gained independence, a woman Minister was included in the Cabinet formed in the then Madras Presidency

content: Following the cancellation of the NEET-UG 2026 paper, the National Testing Agency faces allegations of lax operational capacity, porous cybersecurity, poor crisis communication cancellation again

content: Parteek Yadav was rushed to Civil Hospital in Lucknow, where doctors declared him brought dead; the cause of death is yet to be ascertained

content: Aim is to intensify pressure on the State Government over long-pending demands related to wages, arrears, employee welfare measures, and administrative issues

content: Zilla Panchayat Chief Executive Officer Bhanwar Singh Meena told PDOs to act responsibly in handling drinking water issues in rural areas

content: On December 11, 2023, the Ministry of Home Affairs informed the Supreme Court that the West Bengal government had not adopted the LARR Act 2013, which allows land acquisition for public purposes

title: Education Ministry gives fresh push for implementation of PM-SHRI in Bengal, Kerala, and T.N. post elections
content: Launched in September 2022, this five-year initiative aims to transform over 14,500 schools into “exemplar institutions” that lead the way in implementing the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020; only a limited time is left for the scheme to complete its five-year duration, the Ministry stated in a letter issued to the States

title: Elderly auto driver killed after e-scooter explosion sparks fire in house in Bengaluru
content: The family had plugged in their electric two-wheeler for charging around 11 p.m. before going to sleep. Around 3 a.m., the vehicle allegedly exploded, triggering a fire

title: ‘Don’t want to be part of biased exercise’: Rahul Gandhi records dissent on CBI director’s selection
content: LoP Rahul Gandhi said that despite repeated written requests, he was not provided with the self-appraisal reports or 360-degree reports of the eligible candidates.

title: We will not allow Tamil Thai Vaazhthu to be pushed behind during Governor’s Address, says  Udhayanidhi Stalin

content: On the first day, Mr. Kumar also visited the party office to oversee the arrangements. He took stock of the proceedings and also interacted with the party workers, ministers and the general public present, inquiring about their well-being

title: Demographic shifts and the rise of Hindutva in Kanniyakumari district
content: Kanniyakumari holds a distinctive place in Tamil Nadu’s political history. Despite nearly six decades of Dravidian dominance in the State, it became the first district to elect a Hindutva-backed candidate as early as 1989, when V. Balachandram of Hindu Desam won the Padmanabhapuram constituency

title: Missing Indian man Sandarsh Krishna found dead in Grand Canyon, no cause of death established yet
content: Hiker Sandarsh Krishna, who went missing inside Grand Canyon National Park in Arizona, was found dead. title: Four cubs born to country’s 1st India-born cheetah found dead in Kuno
content: four cubs born to the country's first wild-breeding cheetah were found dead near their den site in Kuno National Park in sheopur district on Tuesday . the cub was found partially consumed, by a monitoring team around 6.30 am during routine surveillance . forest officials suspect the cubes may have fallen prey to another wild animal .
